title_en: Marriage, Divorce, and Commercial Law in Radd al-Muhtar
title_ar: النكاح والطلاق والمعاملات في رد المحتار
content_en:
Ibn 'Abidin's treatment of marriage law in Radd al-Muhtar engages in extraordinary depth with the Hanafi school's distinctive positions, the evidential debates surrounding them, and the practical realities of Ottoman-era family law. His discussion of the woman's right to contract her own marriage without a guardian — a uniquely Hanafi position among the four schools — is particularly thorough, presenting the evidential arguments on both sides before affirming the Hanafi ruling while noting the importance of the guardian's role in practice.

The chapter on divorce (talaq) in Radd al-Muhtar is one of the most elaborate in any Hanafi source. Ibn 'Abidin addresses the different types of divorce: raj'i (revocable), ba'in (irrevocable), bain baynunah kubra (the major irrevocable divorce requiring an intervening marriage), and khul' (mutual dissolution with compensation). He engages with the deeply contested question of the triple talaq pronounced in a single sitting, affirming the Hanafi position that it counts as three — producing the major irrevocable divorce immediately — while noting that some scholars of the school considered this a single revocable divorce.

'Iddah (the waiting period) receives careful treatment. Ibn 'Abidin discusses the 'iddah of different categories of women: the menstruating divorced woman (three complete menstrual cycles), the non-menstruating divorced woman (three lunar months), the pregnant woman (until delivery), and the widow (four months and ten days or until delivery if pregnant). He addresses practical questions about the counting of the 'iddah cycles and the rights and obligations during this period.

Maintenance (nafaqah) is treated as an obligation of the husband toward his wife during the marriage and during the 'iddah of revocable divorce. The Hanafi school specifies that maintenance covers food, clothing, and shelter appropriate to the wife's social standing. Ibn 'Abidin addresses the question of how to assess appropriate maintenance when the husband's and wife's social standings differ significantly, applying the Hanafi principle of averaging.

In commercial transactions, Ibn 'Abidin's most celebrated contributions concern his engagement with the commercial practices of his era. His discussion of mu'amalat addresses the Ottoman commercial contracts, the practices of guilds, and the principles governing market regulation. He engages with the role of the muhtasib (market inspector) in ensuring fair pricing and honest dealing.

Bay' al-wafa' — a transaction in which property is sold with a contractual right of repurchase — was widespread in Ottoman Syria and raises questions of its relationship to mortgage and disguised riba. Ibn 'Abidin analyzes this transaction carefully, affirming the Hanafi position that it is valid as a conditional sale while noting the conditions under which it remains free of riba. His engagement with this question illustrates his method: grounding rulings in classical principles while applying them with sensitivity to contemporary realities.

Ibn 'Abidin closes his commercial law sections by emphasizing the Quranic injunction: 'O you who believe, do not devour each other's wealth unjustly, but only through trade conducted with your mutual consent' (4:29). This verse, he notes, establishes both the permissibility of trade and the ethical framework — mutual consent, absence of injustice — within which all commercial activity must operate.
content_id:
Perlakuan Ibn Abidin terhadap hukum pernikahan dalam Radd al-Muhtar menggali secara luar biasa mendalam posisi-posisi khas madzhab Hanafi, perdebatan-perdebatan bukti di sekelilingnya, dan implikasi praktis untuk situasi yang dihadapi komunitas Muslim Ottoman. Diskusinya tentang persyaratan wali dalam pernikahan Hanafi — di mana madzhab mengizinkan seorang wanita yang sudah dewasa untuk menikahkan dirinya sendiri tanpa persetujuan wali — mencerminkan perlakuan menyeluruh terhadap salah satu perbedaan paling signifikan antara madzhab Hanafi dan yang lainnya.

Pertanyaan-pertanyaan perceraian mendapat perlakuan luas yang sama, dengan Ibn Abidin menangani berbagai jenis perceraian, syarat-syarat kesahannya, konsekuensi-konsekuensinya, dan cara berbagai faktor — kondisi yang ditentukan, keadaan mabuk, paksaan, dan kondisi sakit menjelang kematian — mempengaruhi status hukum dari perceraian yang dinyatakan. Analisis ini sangat penting bagi siapa saja yang bekerja pada pertanyaan-pertanyaan hukum keluarga dalam madzhab Hanafi.

Bagian-bagian hukum komersial dari Radd al-Muhtar mencerminkan keterlibatan Ibn Abidin dengan ekonomi Ottoman yang kompleks. Analisisnya tentang kontrak-kontrak yang digunakan dalam keuangan Ottoman — instrumen keuangan yang dikembangkan oleh para bankir Ottoman dan pedagang untuk memfasilitasi perdagangan jarak jauh — dan tentang status hukum mereka dalam fikih Hanafi menempatkan Radd al-Muhtar di garis depan keterlibatan Islam klasik dengan masalah-masalah keuangan yang terus relevan bagi para ulama hukum keuangan Islam kontemporer.
title_id: Pernikahan, Perceraian, dan Hukum Komersial dalam Radd al-Muhtar
